A skydiver carrying a US flag crashed into an electronic scoreboard during a college football spring game inside Virginia Tech's Lane Stadium in Blacksburg, US, on Saturday. Virginia Tech said the skydiver was safely brought to the ground without injury.
The Israeli army continues demolishing Lebanese homes in villages in southern Lebanon during the ceasefire - Haaretz newspaper

data.title: Sidus Space Announces Pricing of $58.5 Million Registered Direct Offering of Class A Common Stock
data.description: In Cape Canaveral, Florida, on April 19, 2026, Sidus Space, Inc. disclosed the pricing details of a best-efforts registered direct offering. The offering involves 13,453,700 shares of its Class A common stock. Sidus, a renowned space and defense technology firm trading under Nasdaq as SIDU, revealed this crucial update.

data.title: Míla Holding hf. affirms MILA 300929 bond financial obligations report.
data.description: A recent announcement stated that the company's revenue for the last quarter exceeded expectations, reaching $1.5 billion, which is a 10% increase compared to the same period last year. The company also reported a net profit of $200 million during the same quarter, representing a significant growth of 15% year-over-year. Additionally, the number of active users on the platform has soared to 50 million, marking a 20% increase from the previous quarter. The CEO expressed satisfaction with the company's performance and outlined plans for further expansion in the coming months.
Management in Zealand Pharma reported 65 transactions of shares and related securities.

data.title: Bitcoin's halving cycle sees only 97% gains, the weakest ever, compared to over 761% in past cycles. Volatility at 1.75% hints at a significant change.
data.description: Galaxy's Alex Thorn has highlighted Bitcoin's current halving cycle as the weakest ever, with gains limited to 97% compared to over 761% seen in previous cycles. The volatility stands at 1.75%, indicating a significant structural shift in the market.

data.title: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev Opened Up About What Success Means To Him And The Legacy He Would Be 'Excited' To Tell His Grandkids
data.description: Robinhood Markets Inc.'s CEO, Vlad Tenev, of NASDAQ: HOOD, believes that success is about generating significantly more value for the world than for oneself. Tenev emphasized the importance of having "skin in the game" during his appearance on The Knowledge Project Podcast on March 3. He expressed that he would be content if the overall impact on the world surpasses his personal gains. Tenev aims to promote widespread ownership to ensure everyone is actively involved. His vision includes a future where a larger portion of the U.S. population owns key industries, fostering a more educated and engaged society. For more details, check out the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TEAMSTERS AT UNIVERSITY OF CHICAGO MEDICAL CENTER TO HOLD TWO-DAY PRACTICE PICKET
data.description: A group of 2,000 Teamsters are calling for a fair contract at a significant hospital in Chicago. The workers, who are part of the University of Chicago Medical Center (UCMC) and represented by Teamsters Local 743, will begin a two-day practice picket on Monday, April 20 at 5 a.m. The picket is aimed at highlighting the workers' demands for better working conditions.

data.title: In HelloNation, Criminal Defense Attorney Mitchell A. Stone, P.A. Explains Plea Deals
data.description: Jacksonville, Florida - On April 19, 2026, an informative article titled "What Is a Plea Deal?" discusses the implications of a plea deal on a defendant's future. The piece features defense attorney Mitchell A. Stone, P.A., who operates in Jacksonville. The article delves into the significance of plea deals and how they can influence a defendant's legal situation. Attorney Stone provides valuable insights on the subject, shedding light on this crucial aspect of the legal process.
Zealand Pharma introduces incentive programs for Board, Management, and employees for 2026.

data.title: BTC halving cycles showing 'dramatic' underperformance in 2024, with decreasing volatility and upside. Analyst suggests dynamics may change over time.
data.description: Alex Thorn from Galaxy believes that the volatility and potential for gains have decreased after each Bitcoin halving cycle. However, Thorn suggests that this shift in dynamics may not be permanent.

data.title: Kelp DAO breach leads to $291M theft and $6.2B withdrawal panic on Aave, causing DeFi users to face liquidity issues.
data.description: Following a security breach, hackers were able to siphon off $291 million worth of cryptocurrency from the infrastructure associated with Kelp DAO. Subsequently, users of decentralized finance (DeFi) platform Aave encountered difficulties in withdrawing their funds.

data.title: SIRUI Wins "Best of Show", Unveils New Cine Lenses at NAB Show 2026
data.description: At the 2026 NAB Show in Las Vegas on April 19, SIRUI was honored with a "Best of Show" award for its Vision Prime T1.4 Full-Frame Cine Lens Series. Additionally, SIRUI unveiled new focal lengths for the Vision Prime and IronStar lens families. This expansion includes a range of choices for filmmakers and videographers looking to enhance their creative projects.
